title: Inverted Index
body: An inverted index is the most efficient data structure for full-text search. It maps each term in the vocabulary to the list of documents that contain that term, enabling sub-linear query evaluation.

title: Cosine Similarity
body: Cosine similarity measures the cosine of the angle between two non-zero vectors. In information retrieval it is used to compare the TF-IDF vector of a query against every document vector in the collection.

title: P-Norm Operators
body: The Extended Boolean model uses p-norm operators to combine the relevance scores of individual query terms. As p approaches infinity the operators converge to classical AND and OR.
